name: Phillips Abbot
motto: "Alert in the beginning, but negligent in the end."

name: Isadore Abelson
motto: "Much may be said about him on both sides."

name: Howard Teasley Abney
motto: "You come late yet you come."

name: George Dickinson Allen
motto: "The smiles of a pretty woman are the tears of the purse."

name: Ralphiel Phillips Bassett
motto: "I am faultless in disposition and perfect in intellect."

name: George Snider Birch
motto: "By the outward acts we are to judge of the inward thoughts."

name: Elliott Davis Blumenthal
motto: "Faith, that's as well said as if I had sa'd it myself."

name: Mendle Boorstin
motto: "It is well for one to know more than he says."

name: Edwin Nash Broyles
motto: "But, by the Lord, lads, I'm glad you have the money!"

name: John George Burckhardt, Jr.
motto: "I marched the lobby, twirled my stick."

name: Ashton Burford
motto: "It is a great folly to think of being wise alone."

name: William Arnold Burns
motto: "Waste not time for time is the stuff life is made of."

name: Ross Wilson Coker
motto: "The world knows only two, Rome and me!"

name: Clarence Newell Crocker
motto: "What sweet delight a quiet life affords."

name: Roy Cornelius David
motto: "When the body is indisposed, it is in vain that we call on the mind for any strenuous application."

name: Charles Barney Davis
motto: "'Tis thus that on the choice of friends Our good or evil name depends."

name: Charles Milton Davis
motto: "The love of fame usually spurs on the mind."

name: Joel Joseph Davis
motto: "When I err everyone can see it, but not when I lie."

name: Louis Caple English
motto: "A little non-sense now and then is relished by the wisest men."

name: George Henry Firor
motto: "Let him go where no man knoweth him."

name: William Ray Fort
motto: "Be not the first by whom the new are tried; Nor yet the last to lay the old aside."

name: Marvin Augustus Franklin
motto: "Judge not the preacher for he is thy judge."

name: Neil Lee Gillis, Jr.
motto: "Better to reign in hell than serve in Heaven."

name: Robert Jenkins Griffith
motto: "Troubles spring from idleness and grievous toils from needless ease."

name: John McKenzie Gunn
motto: "The smallest hair throws its shadow."

name: John Thomas Hains
motto: "Familiar is his mouth as household words."

name: Edward John Hardin
motto: "The hand that follows intellect can achieve."

name: William Norris Harris
motto: "He says a thousand pleasant things but never says adieu."

name: William Raymond Hastings
motto: "Retire within thyself, and thou wilt discover how small a stock is there."

name: Broadus Jennings Head
motto: "Wisdom is humble that he knows no more."

name: Terrie Nichols Hendricks
motto: "The man is either mad, or he is making verses."

name: Julian Rich Hirshberg
motto: "Knowledge is proud that he has learned so much."

name: Hugh Leslie Hodgson
motto: "Music is well said to be the speech of angels."

name: Champ Holt Holmes
motto: "True merit depends not on the time nor on the fashion."

name: William Linton Howard
motto: "For I am the only one of my friends that I can rely upon."

name: Clark Howell, Jr.
motto: "He flourishes with hereditary honors."

name: Hal Hulsey
motto: "Love spends his all, and still has store."

name: Malcolm Maclean Hutton
motto: "Weakness of mind is the only fault incapable of correction."

name: Charles Wells Jacobson
motto: "But far more numerous was the herd of such, Who think too little and talk too much."

name: Walter Thomas Jenkins
motto: "With just enough learning to misquote."

name: Guy Rudolph Jones
motto: "Nor rural sights alone, but rural sounds exhilarate the spirit."

name: Percival Connally Jones
motto: "The grace of this rich jewel is lost in concealment."

name: Hansel Curtino Kytle
motto: "No talent, but yet a character."

name: Louis Lester
motto: "Other men have acquired fame by industry, but this man by indolence."

name: Bird Little
motto: "To love and to be wise is scarcely granted to the highest."

name: Milward Wyatt Martin
motto: "'Tis not how well an author says; But 'tis how much, that gathers praise!"

name: Frank Oliver Miller
motto: "Let me now seem, what soon I shall be."

name: Stephens Mitchell
motto: "A harmless thunderbolt."

name: Moon, Erastus Franklin
motto: "Sweet through the green leaves shines the moon."

name: McConnell, Bright
motto: "Character is higher than intellect."

name: McGowan, Henri Charbonnier
motto: "Beauty is but skin deep."

name: McLain, William Kenneth
motto: "Looked as if he had walked straight out of the ark."

name: Joseph Nathan
motto: "One can not know everything."

name: Ogden John Olmstead
motto: "Things that are not at all, are never lost."

name: Edward Kinchley Overstreet, Jr.
motto: "In this world a man must either be anvil or hammer."

name: Justus Edwin Owens
motto: "I prefer much rather to be called good than fortunate."

name: Thomas Gerald Peacock, A.B.
motto: "Far must thy researches go Wouldst thou learn the world to know."

name: Scott Hicks Pedrick, B.S.A.
motto: "Dropped from an engel's wing."

name: John William Powell, A.B.
motto: "There is no one who is always in the right but myself."

name: Elmo Ragsdale, B.S.A.
motto: "The talent of human nature runs from one extreme to another."

name: William Morris Redman
motto: "Every man is like the company he is wont to keep."

name: Howard Wade Riley
motto: "Happy am I: from care I'm free, Why aren't they all contented like me."

name: Benjamin Harper Smith
motto: "Pursuit of knowledge under difficulties."

name: Charles Richter Smith
motto: "You know I say just what I think, and nothing more nor less."

name: William Brazeal Sparks, Jr.
motto: "I am myself, and through myself I have become what I am."

name: William Kinnebrew Stanley
motto: "Thy clothes are all the soul thou hast."

name: Claude Thomas Tolbert
motto: "He has learned nothing, and he has forgotten nothing."

name: Ralph Sommerkamp Torbett
motto: "It is much easier to be critical than to be correct."

name: Stephen Bailey Wallace, A.B.
motto: "I attend to other men's business, neglecting my own."

name: Gaines Winningham Walter, A.B.
motto: "Everyone may be proud of his diligence."

name: Charles Douglas Ward, B.S.
motto: "Cultivation is as necessary to the mind, as is food to the body."

name: Frank Crawley Ward, B.S.A.
motto: "He walks with nature and her paths are peace."

name: Audley Alexander Waters
motto: "It wants both beginning and end."

name: Eugene Weatherly
motto: "It is good To lengthen to the last a sunny mood."

name: Robert Wright Wesley
motto: "The word impossible is not in my dictionary."

name: Henry Haynes West
motto: "There are many persons who are esteemed, only because they are not known."

name: Linton Burnside West
motto: "Nothing stings more deeply than the loss of money."

name: George Stevens Whitehead
motto: "When night hath set her silver lamp on high then is the time for study."

name: Courtland Simmons Winn, Jr.
motto: "I never have desired That the same bark should grow on every tree."

name: James Raiford Wood
motto: "I'll make me glorious by my pen."

name: James Fletcher Woodall
motto: "Every bird thinks his own nest handsome."

name: Homer Wright, Jr.
motto: "Empty heads console with empty sounds."

name: W. Y. Atkinson
clubs: President of the Senior Law Class

name: D. L. Plaster
clubs: Vice-President of the Senior Law Class

name: A. S. Camp
clubs: Secretary of the Senior Law Class

name: A. G. Tillinghast
clubs: Poet of the Senior Law Class

name: T. S. Candler
clubs: Historian of the Senior Law Class

name: C. L. H. Pigue
clubs: Chaplain of the Senior Law Class

name: Grover Cleveland Anderson
motto: "He who has imagination without learning has wings but no feet."

name: Clifton Olin Barrett
motto: "I am not in the roll of common men."

name: Leonard Gaston Broughton
motto: "Those who cross the sea change their climate but not their mind."

name: Albert Sidney Camp
motto: "Like two single gentlemen, rolled into one."

name: Thomas Slaughter Candler
motto: "Then clasp me around the neck once more and give me one more kiss."

name: David Lee Cotton
motto: "For I was born for other things."

name: Edgar Brown Dunlap
motto: "The clatter of arms drowns the voice of the law."

name: Chestley Vernon Elliott
motto: "A man can not wive and thrive at the same time."

name: Grady Hill Gaston
motto: "To make another person hold his tongue, be you first silent."

name: Harry McClesky Hawkins
motto: "Nothing can come from nothing."

name: Wesley Houser
motto: "I to myself am dearer than a friend."

name: Henry Grady Howard
motto: "There is no departing from the words of the law."

name: Gus Alvin Huddleston
motto: "None but himself can be his parallel."

name: Covington Drane Jenkins
motto: "What ignorance there is in human mind."

name: William Hicks Key
motto: "Prosperity makes few friends."

name: Ben Link
motto: "In general those who have nothing to say contrive to spend the longest time in doing it."

name: Peace Robert Matthews
motto: "A wise man sometimes changes his mind, a fool never."

name: S. Thomas Memory
motto: "Ambition is no cure for love."

name: Clyde Mather McClure
motto: "There is a higher law than the constitution."

name: Alexander McDougald
motto: "Age bears away with it all things, even the powers of mind."

name: David Knox McKamy
motto: "A good lawyer is a bad neighbor."

name: Earle Van Ness Norman
motto: "Oh! What a useful secret it is to be able to lie to the purpose."

name: Howell Benajah Peacock
motto: "A pleasing countenance is a silent recommendation."

name: Charles Louis Hill Pigue
motto: "Old politicians chew on wisdom past And totter on in bus'ness to the last."

name: Lewis Amheim Pinkussohn, B.L.
motto: "A mugwump is a person educated beyond his intellect."

name: Claude Cleveland Pittman, A.B., B.L.
motto: "Each man likes his way the best And so the world with fools is blessed."

name: Daniel Liddell Plaster, B.L.
motto: "It is pleasant to play the fool in the proper place, but not all the time."

name: John Clifford Pratt, B.L.
motto: "The foremost man of all this world."

name: Albon Williams Reed
motto: "Some of the wisdom will get in anyhow."

name: Augustine Sams
motto: "Bid them come forth and hear me."

name: Nathaniel Cornelius Spence
motto: "He makes no friends who never had a foe."

name: Donald Peyton Starr
motto: "Until I truly loved, I was alone."

name: William Theodore Swanson
motto: "What a fine man hath your tailor made you".

name: Arthur Gideon Tillinghast
motto: "It is strange so great a statesman should be so sublime a poet."

name: Edward J. A. Trotter
motto: "Rather than be less, Cared not to be at all."

name: William David Turner
motto: "Wit does not take the place of knowledge."

name: John Lewis Tye, Jr.
motto: "Three-fifths of him genius and two-fifths sheer fudge."

name: Sidney Johnson Underwood
motto: "We enter the world alone, we leave 't alone."

name: Edward Grady Weathers
motto: "Born merely for the purpose of digestion."

name: Johnie Lenoir Westmoreland
motto: "A rare bird on earth."

name: Rosier Bunyan Wingate
motto: "I am the very slave of circumstances."

name: GROVER CLEVELAND ANDERSON
description: Brutus said Caesar was ambitious, and Brutus was an honorable man, but could Brutus have had the unimpressionistic pleasure of G. C. Anderson's acquaintance his idea of an ambitious man would have reached magnitudes insuperable. You see Anderson is reading law, not to be master of its science nor a student of its intricacies, but merely for the purpose of using it as a criterion in his political transactions. Now Anderson's ambition is to gain fame through his political ability to deceive people; he intends to rapidly become solicitor of his judicial circuit, then judge and of course with all ease reach the supreme head in the nation's capitol. A jump from the "jerk water" town of Wrens to the city of Washington is a jump not impossible within the confines of his intoxicated imagination, nor within the scope of his superstructural ambition. He is all ambition and misunderstanding and as the class judges his ability by the inconsistent thought of his mind and the absurdity of his terriffic fire of questions then we can't see how he can make this contemplated jump, but as an alternative advise him to jump on his ambition and kill it and begin a more serious line of thought.

name: WILLIAM YATES ATKINSON
description: All hail our president—yes, Bill helped put Newnan on the map. Whether Newnan is appreciative remains to be seen. From all indications she should be, for "Bill", who is within mighty close range of the "rifle pit" is very seldom hit by the "dum-dums" of the Kaiser. We can't understand though why Bill insists that the day set for Medical Jur'sprudence exam is a fitting day to get sick. Perhaps the Juris Prudence was uppermost in Bill's thought. But mind, we don't know, we just throw this out as a suggestion.

name: C. O. BARRETT
description: For two years Chocolate has led a life of unalloyed fear. Fear for what yesterday's marks were and fear for what tomorrow's remarks would be. It is plain that the Great Oracle wasn't always pleased by the way "Choc" took its d'etates. It is safe to say that the meaning of the word "property" is deeply imbedded in the cranium of said Barrett. It has, indeed, become an incorporeal hereditament. But with all that "Choc" will make a lawyer and a good one just so long as there is a justice court in which to practice.

name: LEN G. BROUGHTON, JR.
description: We know not the motive—the fact is—Broughton has as big a bootlick as any pseudo lawyer that has ever passed through the egg stage. Due to said affection Len's sailing

name: A. S. CAMP
description: And he's from Newnan. We never realized Newnan was half so large. No, Sid, we don't doubt you in the least, we believe that that Utopian metropolis is the veritable Eden of Georgia and the garden spot of the world. We had never heard of "Newnan" until that magic word trickled inadvertently from your unguarded lips. Then there was sunshine and the roses bloomed and the world looked—but let's forget the place (if we can) and talk about the man. Sid, (and this is absolutely true) is the biggest man in the class (weight, 250 lbs., or thereabout). But he's got a head—one big enough to steer his frame and steer it right. We ain't at all worried about Newnan's favorite son.

name: THOS. S. CANDLER
description: Now "sposin" this humble inkslinger should put something in this biography,—something that he knows to be the truth and wants to tell the public,—but something Thomas "Sposin" Candler doesn't like, well Thomas "Sposin" would use his blue pencil (he's an editor). I know a lot of things but I just can't tell them. To be mild,—Thomas "Sposin" is a politician of the Charlie Murphy variety and besides he is a pretty good lawyer. Either one is O. K. by itself, but mixed together, its dangerous for an unsuspecting public (I guess I had better say something complimentary or he'll scratch that out), but laying all jokes aside, Tom will get there, for his steam not only blows the whistle but also turns the wheels.

name: D. LEE COTTON
motto: "Seldom he smiles; and smiles in such a sort, As if he mocked himself, and scorned his spirits, That could be moved to smile at anything."
description: Possibly this gloomy outlook on this huge imposture of a world grew and thrived on account of the fact that D. Lee had one roommate i. e. Gaston, for a whole year. Said Gaston can talk the horns off a "dilemma" and still say nothing. Hence Lee, due to habit, has nothing much to say either when he is called on or when he is not. But that is not all, Lee occasionally gives the ladies a treat and, I believe, is classed as one of the "dream dancers" who make the "Castle Walk".

name: EDGAR B. DUNLAP
description: God made Heaven and earth in six days and on the seventh He rested. Shortly after He made an Eden in Athens and called it the University Campus. Twenty-four hours after the completion of this work Ed Dunlap moved his trunk from Gainesville and here he has been camping ever since. To describe this fixture would send Bernard Shaw into a dictionary on a search for adjectives. All this poor writer can say is that Ed is as

name: CHESTLEY VERNON ELLIOTT
description: Here's at least a queer combination. We have heard of lawyers who write insurance on the side; we have heard of lawyer loan-sharks; we have heard of lawyer politicians, but we plead ignorance when it comes to lawyer undertakers. That is we plead this before we met the aforesaid Vernon. It is said that Vernon is going to be a district attorney in some "Jerk water" town in Georgia. Some place where he can procure a conviction and hang his man just to have the pleasure of burying him. It's a pleasant thought, but Vernon dotes on funerals, in fact the harder the undertaking the more Mr. Elliott, lawyer,—expert embalmer,—benedict, likes it.

name: GRADY HILL GASTON
description: Is there anything in a name? I wonder. The first of the syllables of Grady's last cognomen is strictly illustrative of five fourths of Grady. We wouldn't dare deny Grady's ability (fact is, he wouldn't let us) but we would be willing to bet our last year's straw lid against a dope that "Gas pipe" would illuminate darkest Africa for a century,—that is if he lived long enough. His illuminating phraseology, however, doesn't seem to throw much light on cases put before him by the Grand Doge. But we'll hand it to you, Grady, if talking were mole hills, you'd be the Woolworth Building.

name: HARRY M. HAWKINS
description: Oh Apollo Bellvedere, would'st tell me of your charms? You, who lead the Germans and the ladies hearts alarm? Stop and tell us between dances of the many love sick glances, of the piercing cupid's lances that have failed to find their mark. Leader of the Germans, stop and tell us of the war. Is the Crown Prince on the war path in the region of the law? Stop and tell us, between glasses, of the many dreary classes, of the many many passes that have failed to get their mark.

name: WESLEY HOUSER
description: Many peaches have been shipped away from Fort Valley and one of the best looking (in his own estimation) of these peaches is the aforesaid Wesley. He carries a mirror in his pocket all the time and between shakes in Mr. Morris's class he contemplates with great satisfaction his beautiful countenance. Aside from this distinguishing feature he is renowned for the fact that every now and then he condescends to associate with one or two mere mortals; that he has never explored the inside of the Library; that he is said to have vast learning which he has never displayed and that the ladies one and all pine away when he does not deign to grant them a glance now and then. The Justice Court will gain a likely specimen if some widow doesn't rope him first.

name: HENRY GRADY HOWARD
description: What eyes! what temperament! methought when I did gaze into those orbs thou wert "an" poet,—nay "a" actor, but ne'er would I believe thou wert a paltry piddling practitioner.

name: GUSTAVUS ADOLPHUS HUDDLESTON
description: Gus is without doubt the most graceful fellow in the Senior Class and if ever a great baseball player came to the University with a self proclaimed "rep", it was Gus. We won't mention how many games he has pitched, however, since our space is limited. Gus is really somewhat handicapped since he is forced to room with Jenkins and for that reason we won't mention some of his performances before the Great Oracle; but we must mention his one great political success. He ran for President of Demosthenian, for President of Jeffersonian, for Impromptu Debater, and every time the machine ruthlessly crushed his young ambition, but, at last, with the able assistance of the man who "Put the skids under Anderson" he defeated the machine and was elected sheriff in a blaze of Glory. With it all Gus is somewhat of a lawyer.

name: COVINGTON DRANE JENKINS
description: We don't know what to say about this pestiferous piece of protoplasm. Not that he is so annoying to his classmates but he must be annoying to himself. He is always kidding himself along by telling himself that JENKINS is the very best man (in every respect) that ever trod shoe leather or wore a cowlick in the middle of his forehead. We agree with you "Jenks" you are a good man, and, honest, we won't forget it even if you don't remind us. What worries us is, what would happen if Jenkins as he is, should doubt Jenkins as he thinks he is. Ten to one there would be a decided slump on the stock exchange of Auburn graduates, "Jenkins as he thinks he is" selling at fifty per cent below par.

name: WILLIAM HICKS KEY
description: Another Bill! Ye Gods! Why name them Bill. Is it to remind one of the first of the month? Well, anyway, this is a very rare sort of Bill (not one of the $15 variety). This Bill is a member of the Georgia legislature. The registrar said that he knew "that all representatives needed a guardian but Bill Key was the first one he had seen to admit it". We expect Bill to do great things at the capitol. It is rumored that he will introduce a bill to turn the turnpike into an auto race track. We aren't sure about this, but we think that's what the bill board at Jasper County said. Laying all jokes aside, put your bills on Bill, he's a winner.

name: BEN LINK
description: The writer can't pull that joke about the "missing link", because he isn't. He is mostly always there. Nor would we have missed him if he wasn't. His most notorious ac-

name: PEARCE R. MATTHEWS
clubs: Married Men's Club of the Senior Law class
description: Mr. Pearce R. Matthews hails from the Lone Star state and boasts of the fact that he is wild and woolly. On entering the University, Matty joined the G. O. P., and at the cigarette banquet given by said party in February made a speech which is in substance as follows: "Lord of Lords and King of Kings, I have a son, a noble son indeed he is, even more so than his father. I will send him to the University to take law and will of course advise him to join the G. O. P.'s." Matty has made a wonderful record in the University. It is said that he is the only man in the class who has been able to catch every point of a certain Professor, no matter from what angle flung. To say that he will make a good lawyer is to waste energy, nothing more need be said than to watch the firm of Gaston, Dunlap and Matthews.

name: S. THOMAS MEMORY
description: The Thomas of old was a doubting Thomas but this Thomas is an interrogating Thomas. He has no limit to the number of questions per minute, his record now stands at 100. Thomas is in love with a certain little girl of Thomasville, or else with his mother-in-law, you cannot tell which by his conversation. D. Lee Cotton says that Memory is the only man he has ever conversed with that could ask enough questions to absolutely prevent him from expounding his philosophy (a great pity indeed it is). Where this Thomas will practice law no one knows or dares conjecture.

name: ALEC McDOUGALD
description: Alec Mac is a "little man". He has to be entitled a "little man" in order for those who know him to be impressed with his identity in comparison with his own impression of his identity. Mac has a real defense against any bona fide purchaser for value "with notice", against his own opinion. We give him credit for his opinion and the facts upon which he bases it, but the man is yet to be found to give him credit for the manner in which he delivers it. This is Mac—he come up to you and begins his imperialistic tone of monotonous discourse by making you believe that he knows every thing to be known and that you, poor little you, are the biggest fool yet upon which he has condescended even to gaze. But nobody takes Mac seriously but himself, and as he doesn't count outside of himself, where's the harm? You're right, Mac; you're good!

name: D. K. McKAMY
description: He, "indeed, is as mild mannered a man as ever scuttled a ship or cut a throat". Clever by instinct; quick through learning, and artful by practice, it is said of Mac that he can make a bronze statue donate coppers for the purpose of buying sun gardens for the Eskimos or watering pots for deep sea mammals. Of course, we wouldn't swear that this is where the money went; but Mac said that was what he did with it, so we have...

name: C. C. PITTMAN
description: C square, long known at Emory as 'Boss' Pittman, is one of the great (?) seven who added themselves to the roll this year. It did not take 'Boss' Pittman long to find out that when it comes to bossing there is at least one who is his equal. In fact soon after the moment when he told the real Boss what a great criminal lawyer he intended to be, the aforesaid C square knew that he still had a little to learn. However, he may yet realize his dream if he can only induce enough criminals to help him. Certain it is that the gallery will rave over him; but if he intends to follow politics, we would advise that he be more careful in making out his ballot than he was in the Athletic Association election. Taken all in all Pittman is a queer specimen and it is doubtful if his like is to be found anywhere. Here's hoping anyway.

name: CLYDE MATHER McCLURE
description: 'Mac' is generally known as the official greaser of the Candler Hall steam roller in the 'Old Jeffersonian'. He is a product of Washington and Lee, assisted by the Normal School (the same one that turned loose the Gentleman from Jasper). He, along with the great Pittman and a few others, cast their lot with us this year and from all accounts has survived until the present. He is said to chew more tobacco and to know more Medical Jurisprudence than even Pigue and it is doubtful if a stauncher Mugwump could be found anywhere. It is reported also that he is the author of the famous song 'Who put the Skids under Anderson?' (Jenkins hasn't admitted this yet, however.) Ask him why he and Candler went to the Southern depot on a certain night in February.

name: J. C. PRATT
description: (J. C. stands for Julius Caesar.) 'Books, books everywhere and not a sucker to buy,' which is Julius Caesar's lament from morning to night, that is when Kittie lets him get alone for a moment. J. C. thinks that he is the first, last and only ladies man that ever came from the classic walls of Lawrenceville to rend the hearts of fair maidens. Come to think about it, he has the same opinion of the said Pratt in almost everything else, however, he hasn't yet explained how he intends to carry his claim cases up in a grip sack. Besides the aforementioned accomplishments, Col. Pratt has a number of other distinctions. He is quite a politician in Demosthenian, he always stands among the first in the big eaters contest, he is an ardent admirer of a Certain Starr, and he rooms with the Gentleman from Jasper, the Hon. Wm. Hicks Key. Certainly Pratt is bound to succeed as a lawyer.

name: HOWELL BENAJAH PEACOCK
description: When it becomes necessary to write up the peculiarities of people and you strike a man who hasn't any, what must you do? Howell is without doubt the most common, ordinary,

name: Pigue, Charles Louis Hill
description: We could write volumes about 'Piggy', but general reader, we have sufficient compassion on you to spare you the ordeal. If you happen to be interested concerning the said C. L. H. Pigue seek him out and ask him to tell you anything you'd like to know, he will do it. Trust to Pigue. Be it the fourth dimension or the 'if' of an arthropod, he will tell you and satisfy himself that he is right. Besides being the biggest 'Wardheeler' and the biggest Freshman in College, Pigue is the happiest father we know.

name: Pinkussohn, Louis
description: 'Pin-Cushion', as he insists on being called, is one of the few great men of the Senior Class. (The others being Sid Camp and Fess Plaster). He says that he has been consulted by the Chancellor about running the University more times than even Jacobson or Sam Wiley, and that is saying a lot. But, aside from being the chief aid in running the University he has other distinctions. He sits on the front seat next to Henry Grady Howard; he and Jacobson are the leading actors in College; he helped Hendricks and Mitchell get out the only issue of the Georgian; he is the chief friend and personal adviser of Johnny Westmoreland, and last but not least, he is the editor of these so-called Memoirs.

name: Plaster, \"Fess\"
description: Damon and Pythias, Jonathon and David, Fess and Sprog, these are the three renowned friendships of history. They say, 'Like seeks like'. I wouldn't say in this case, for fear of hurting 'Fess' or 'Sprog's' feelings. Well, no matter how it happens but 'Fess' sticks to 'Sprog' worse than a mustard plaster would to an Australian gnat hound. But that is not the worst of it; they 'Stick' anybody who comes within 'Touching' distance, that is, all except the Exalted Ruler, he always gets away with the bacon.

name: Norman, \"Sprog\"
description: Damon and Pythias, Jonathon and David, Fess and Sprog, these are the three renowned friendships of history. They say, 'Like seeks like'. I wouldn't say in this case, for fear of hurting 'Fess' or 'Sprog's' feelings. Well, no matter how it happens but 'Fess' sticks to 'Sprog' worse than a mustard plaster would to an Australian gnat hound. But that is not the worst of it; they 'Stick' anybody who comes within 'Touching' distance, that is, all except the Exalted Ruler, he always gets away with the bacon.

name: Reed, Albon
description: 'Bunk' has learned one lesson (if no other) during his stay in the law department and that is that he may outclass the Kaiser on the golf course, but he can't golf out the Kaiser's course in class. 'Bunk' sometimes comes to class and takes a back seat, yawns, and then waits complacently for the big guns to start their deadly fire. Ammunition never gives out and the gunner is always on the job. Woe to 'Bunk'!

name: AUGUSTINE SAMS.
description: Here is a guy that minds his own business, keeps his nose to the grindstone and makes it his business to find out as much about the Law as ordinary eyesight and Morpheus will allow him. Gus has found to his sorrow that he isn't a very good politician. But we commend him for that. The most peculiar thing about Gus is that he promises to be a peachy good lawyer, and yet, he hasn't even learned to chew tobacco.

name: NATHANIEL SPENCE.
clubs: firm of Spence, Starr and Swanson
description: "Nat", as he is affectionately called by his hosts of admirers, can study more to the square inch between eight forty-five and eight fifty-five and less at all other times than any man in the Senior Class. He seems especially gifted in swinging his cane, wearing his derby on the side of his head, and talking of the amount of time he spends running up and down Peachtree in an automobile. He is a charter member of the firm of Spence, Starr and Swanson whose specialty is Bankruptcy. However in spite of these manifold advantages, we predict that he will win every case in which he opposes Willcox and Sid Camp. Laying all jokes aside, though, Spence is a good all round fellow and if he doesn't succeed he will think he has, so what's the difference?

name: DONALD P. STARR.
clubs: firm of Starr, Spence and Swanson
description: It was said, "His luminous countenance draws and attracts the members of the fair sex as a lamp in the darkness allures a moth." He is repeatedly getting telephone calls from some fair admirer who delights in mystifying by refusing to disclose her identity. He also runs to the gay Metropolis of Madison, Ga., occasionally, sometimes goes with Ben Link in his Ford, taking "Skeets" Trotter along as a guide. He is a great stump speaker too. He made himself famous by making a brilliant speech (altogether impromptu) to the passengers of the Central on the way from Madison to Athens, on one occasion, his subject being "A Little More Of It." In class however, — "Ahem! — er — will you repeat the question Fessor?" But after a repetition of the question and a long pause, he breaks the silence and "Comes Back". Furthermore it is admitted that he has the brightest future of any member in his class. He will form a partnership with Ben Link.

name: WILLIAM T. SWANSON.
clubs: firm of Starr, Spence and Swanson
description: William "T." Swanson of Blairsville, the ladies man of our class, is a member of the firm of Starr, Spence and Swanson, and some firm indeed it is. Bill says that he got a "dirty deal" in his course on Wills in that Prof. Nix did not ask him a single question that he could answer without having read the text. On entering the University, Bill was asked the question by the Dean, "Mr. Swanson, what's your name?" "William T. Swanson". "Well, William T., didn't they have anything else to name you?" "I hereby christen you William Tecumseh Swanson". But to do justice to Bill you will have to say that he is some lawyer.

name: ARTHUR G. TILLINGHAST.
description: Oh, enigmas! Of all the puzzles our friend from Connecticut is the hardest to solve. Which is the real man, — Arthur the poet, Arthur the lawyer, or Arthur the auto accessory salesman? The writer leaves the first question to sympathetic and indul-

name: E. J. A. TROTTER
description: "Skeets" is the most peculiar specimen of a pseudo lawyer that has ever slept thru a twelve o'clock class. He spends his entire time trying to make music by bringing his teeth in contact with some body else's pencil and at the same time makes his head give out a sound resembling that made by striking an empty gourd (a very easy matter by the way). In fact, he is filled with music and the only time that he doesn't inflict it upon the long suffering public is when the Grand Exalted Potentate asks him a question and then he turns and squirms at the rate of a hundred thousand revolutions per second and issues a perfect stream of "wait a minute Professors." However, he gets by somehow in spite of the fact that he rooms with Sid Camp and hails from the same town as Pennington.

name: WILLIAM D. TURNER
motto: "Full well they laughed, with counterfeited glee, At all his jokes, for many a joke had he."
description: This by-product of Jesup is indeed the comedian of the Senior Law class. To him belongs the sole and despotic right to say what he pleases, when he pleases and how he pleases. And to say the least, he pleases, for everybody laughs. Bill is a wit—one of that rare kind who sees a joke in everything but himself. The only disappointing feature throughout Bill's legal career was that the Exalted Emperor could never see the point. Hence, Bill must have realized that a joke ill taken is a sad reality—and law is a serious business—but I can't help quoting from Goldsmith; Oliver must have thought of Bill Turner when he wrote,—

name: JOHN L. TYE
description: Tye did not cast his hat into Mr. Morris's ring until the race was half over; but he has certainly done faithful service since the aforesaid casting. During the Fall term he attended four classes at one and the same time and passed all of them up, that he did not flunk. He says more in the longest time and with less of the truth in it than even Gaston; he goes to see the ladies and studies for exams with Starr; he looks very much like Pinkussohn and dresses a la Brummel; he has a charter seat at Poss's Place, the Q room, and the Colonial, and was seen in the Library once. Besides this he promises to make a good lawyer and get married very early.

name: S. J. UNDERWOOD
description: Brethren, let us pause. We are treading on hallowed ground. Mr. Underwood (no relation to Oscar W. by the way) is the only man in the knowledge of the writer who ever went through the trials and tribulations of a two year law course and came out unscathed and dignified. Oh solemn one, tell us the secret of your success. We have seen you "buck" the Lord Chief Justice and make him change his decree; we have seen you

name: EDWARD GRADY WEATHERS
description: Hon. Edward Grady Weathers, known to all as the "Jedge", landed in Athens in the fall of 1914 with lofty aspirations, entered politics, joined the G. O. P.'s and selected Mr. Weinkle as a roommate. Realizing that he could not master all the law in the brief time spent here he decided to specialize on appeals, claims and certiorari, especially the latter, insisting that the proper way to carry them up to the Superior Court was by sending them up in a balloon, skyrocket or by taking them up in his gripsack. After his election as president of the Jeffersonian, miraculous indeed it was, the only explanation or apology the Jedge could offer for the same was that some men achieve greatness, others have it thrust upon them, while still others are born great. All those who know the Jedge realize that he belongs to the last class. His prospects in the legal profession are indeed bright, if he can only keep sun-shine in his grip.

name: JOHN L. WESTMORELAND
enrolltime: been at Georgia only one year
description: Here is a peaceable, good natured sort of "cuss" who takes everything as a joke with one exception—Himself. And this is the secret of Johnny's popularity. Though he has been at Georgia only one year, still he is liked by all who know him, and the best of it is, everybody knows him. Nor are Johnny's activities merely centered within the Campus. Law is an all absorbing study for some people but John seems to think that young girls can't get a finish at a finishing school unless they become proficient in basketball. "Ain't he grand?" whispered one of his "Lucy" pupils to her teammates. "Uhhuh", was the answer, "How did he ever graduate from Mercer?"

name: R. B. WINGATE
description: For fear that his wife may see this and that it might cause domestic infelicity in that happy home, we really don't dare to tell the truth concerning the secretary of the Bludwine Company. Besides, he gave the Pandora an Ad and that is enough to insure anyone from a Knock. R. Bunyan is some Bunyan when it comes to sitting on the front seat and escaping destruction day after day by a mere miracle, and yet, that is only one of his rare gifts. He bulldozed the class into electing him president and then immediately ran off and got married and followed this feat by giving us all a free Bludwine (wish he would do it again). We don't think he intends to practice law and so won't mention his ability in that line; but if he and Gaston could only form a partnership it would be a howling success.

name: Owen, W. H.
major: Senior Pharmacy Class, School of Pharmacy

name: Horovitz, I.
major: Senior Pharmacy Class, School of Pharmacy
description: The historian of the Pharmacy Class.

name: William Gant Barrow
motto: "Out of sight, out of mind."

name: Isodore Horovitz
motto: "After I have named the man, I need say no more."

name: Julius Edgar Jenkins
motto: "Nothing is more useful than silence."

name: Wayland Hoyt Owen
motto: "I am not, what I once was."

name: Copy Right Smythe
description: Orphan and sole heir to wealthy banker.

name: Arry Harnold
description: Diplomatic hypocrite, secret boss of the "Grasping Paw," notorious as "The Cat" for his pussy-footed work; Smythe's guardian.

name: Heander Nendrix
description: Member of the "Grasping Paw," accomplice and understudy of Harnold.

name: Earl E. Crook
description: A would-be politician and satellite of the Terrier.

name: Gee Peruna Darnedassone
description: Wife of the Terrier.

name: Lizzy Con Geals
description: Buxom lass with whom Smythe has become enamored.

name: Keneth Complain
description: Janitor and minor member of the "Grasping Paw."

name: Considerable Howl
description: Reporter for the paper, "Institution."

name: Sam Doem Slily
description: Undertaker.

name: Caesar Napoleon Blumingfule
description: Policeman.
